pr.title: Use CharacterKind enums for all c_kind values
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Replaced raw numeric c_kind character identifiers and loose integer prototypes with CharacterKind-aware names and types across fighter forward declarations, game-mode setup code, player APIs, and audio APIs. The PR also documented the CharacterKind enum indices in hex and split an opaque gm_8049E548 padding field into named character-kind and color arrays where the access patterns were evident.
what_changed: Added explicit hex index comments to every CharacterKind enumerator in src/melee/ft/forward.h, making values such as CKIND_CREZYH at 0x1E, CHKIND_SANDBAG at 0x1F, CHKIND_POPO at 0x20, and CHKIND_NONE at 0x21 easier to verify against raw constants.
Replaced raw c_kind constant 0x21 with CHKIND_NONE in gm_8016795C when initializing PlayerInitData.
Replaced raw c_kind constant 0x1E with CKIND_CREZYH in gm_17C0.c.
Replaced raw c_kind constant 0x1F with CHKIND_SANDBAG in gmhomerun.c for the Sandbag player setup.
Changed gm_80163298's first parameter declaration from int to CharacterKind in gm_1601.h.
Changed lbAudioAx_80026E84 from taking int c_kind to CharacterKind c_kind in both lbaudio_ax.c and lbaudio_ax.h, adding the ft forward include needed by the header.
Changed Player_SetPlayerCharacter's value parameter from s32 to CharacterKind in player.c and player.h.
Renamed gm_801B0620 and gm_801B0664 parameter arg1 to c_kind in gm_1B03.c while keeping the stored field assignment behavior unchanged.

Refined gm_8049E548_t in gm_1B03.h from an 8-byte pad_0 field to char c_kind[4] followed by u8 x4[4], reflecting how gm_1BA8.c indexes the first four bytes as character kinds and the next four bytes as another per-player byte array.
Removed several M2C_FIELD accesses in gm_1BA8.c by using named fields: gm_8049E548.c_kind[arg0], gm_8049E548.x4[arg0], and gm_8049E548.unk_9.
Documented c_kind-related fields in gm/types.h and mn/types.h as using CharacterKind or CKIND_* values without changing their byte-sized storage type.
smart_moves: Kept byte-sized storage for c_kind fields where structs appear to require s8/u8 layout, while using CharacterKind in function signatures where type intent can be expressed without risking struct size changes.
Converted magic constants only where the CharacterKind enum values were already defined and directly corresponded to observed values, avoiding speculative new enum entries.
Added enum index comments instead of assigning explicit numeric values to every enumerator, improving auditability while preserving the existing implicit enum ordering.
Used the repeated indexed access pattern in gm_1BA8.c to justify splitting gm_8049E548_t's opaque pad_0 into c_kind[4] and x4[4], then removed M2C_FIELD calls in favor of normal struct field access.
Propagated type intent consistently through matching declarations and definitions, such as lbAudioAx_80026E84 and Player_SetPlayerCharacter, so headers and implementations stayed synchronized.
decomp_lessons: When a field is semantically an enum but stored as s8 or u8 in a packed/game struct, prefer documenting the field as using that enum and typing API parameters as the enum rather than blindly changing the field storage type.

Raw constants in player initialization code are good candidates for enum replacement when the enum table includes stable value comments; this improves searchability and reduces future off-by-one or identity mistakes.
Adding value comments to enums is useful in decomp work because many callsites still expose raw hex or decimal constants before all references are fully typed.

M2C_FIELD cleanup is safest when the struct layout can be inferred from multiple matching indexed accesses; here, offsets 0..3 and 4..7 were separated into arrays instead of remaining as one opaque pad.
Header include dependencies should follow newly exposed types: lbaudio_ax.h needed melee/ft/forward.h once its public prototype used CharacterKind.
naming_conventions: Use CharacterKind for character identity parameters and return-local variables instead of generic int, s32, or tmp names when the value is a c_kind.
Use c_kind as the field or parameter name for CharacterKind-like values, matching existing PlayerInitData naming.

CharacterKind enumerators use CKIND_* for most characters but existing special entries include CHKIND_SANDBAG, CHKIND_POPO, CHKIND_NONE, and CHKIND_MAX; this PR preserved those existing spellings rather than renaming them.

Historical/internal names in CharacterKind comments are retained, such as CKIND_MARS for Marth, CKIND_SEAK for Sheik, CKIND_CLINK for Young Link, CKIND_EMBLEM for Roy, CKIND_CREZYH for Crazy Hand, and CKIND_GKOOPS for Giga Bowser.
Temporary variable tmp was renamed to kind when holding the result of gm_801BF648 for assignment into PlayerInitData.c_kind.
Opaque struct bytes should be renamed only as far as evidence supports: gm_8049E548.pad_0 became c_kind[4] and x4[4], while unknown fields like unk_9 kept generic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced M2C_FIELD macro accesses with direct struct fields after splitting gm_8049E548_t into named arrays at the same offsets.
Used enum-typed variables and parameters without changing underlying assignments, a low-risk readability improvement that should not alter generated code in normal C enum ABI usage.
Preserved implicit enum ordering while annotating offsets, avoiding codegen or ABI risk from explicit initializer edits.
Kept PlayerInitData.c_kind as s8 with documentation instead of changing it to CharacterKind, likely avoiding struct layout and signedness churn.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
No human PR body text was included in the provided slice, so rationale is inferred from the title and diff only.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining assignments to .c_kind using raw numeric constants and replace them with CharacterKind enumerators where values are confirmed.
Search for function parameters named c_kind that are still typed int, s32, s8, or u8 and decide whether the API boundary can safely use CharacterKind.
Audit CharacterKind spelling consistency, especially existing CHKIND_* versus CKIND_* entries, before attempting any enum rename because this PR intentionally preserved current names.
Look for remaining M2C_FIELD accesses around gm_8049E548_t or adjacent structs that can be replaced after layout evidence is strong.
Check whether gm_801BF648 and related functions can return CharacterKind instead of s8 without affecting matching or ABI expectations.
